prompt_authoring.overview: Generate external LLM prompts that preserve YAML validity and human-readable Markdown narrative.

ordering_requirements: Render blocks in local cluster order: section then that section's occurrences, then next section.
Occurrence block order should follow each section.occurrences list.
Keep the registry block at the end.
narrative_requirements: After each section YAML block, include one or more Markdown paragraphs summarizing intent and context.
After each occurrence YAML block, include at least one sentence explaining claim/evidence.
Avoid YAML-only documents intended for human reading.

behavior: If explicit references exist in input, generate structured references.
If no references exist, references section/occurrences may be omitted.
Do not invent citation identifiers or URLs.

required: Map source links to refs.sources[] with id/uri/role/description.
Connect claims to source entities via supporting_evidence and optional target.
notes: Core rules should apply even when no template is provided.
Unknown citation subfields should be ignored by consumers unless template-aware.

required: Create IE-REF-* entities and OCC-REF-* occurrences for major references (report page, PDF, DOI when present).
Create a references section containing OCC-REF-*.
Keep OCC-*-CITATION as aggregate and link to IE-REF-* via supporting_evidence.
If template defines reference profile, apply extra fields in addition to core standard.
Optionally include template source in refs.sources with role=template.

system: You are generating IdeaMark v1.0.3 documents for both machines and humans.
Keep IDs stable and references resolvable.
task: Write narrative text immediately after each section and occurrence YAML block.
Render section-scoped local clusters: section then listed occurrences.
quality_gate: Must include human-readable narrative adjacent to structure blocks.
